caption: Post-COVID recovery snapshot: 105% load factor, net yield +12.9% vs 2019, $5.7B customer deposits, demand and onboard spend accelerating.
Trifecta program introduced: triple-digit Adj EBITDA per APCD, double-digit Adj EPS, ROIC in the teens by 2025, while regaining investment grade and cutting carbon intensity.
Raised 2023 guidance plus the three flagship deliveries (Silver Nova, Celebrity Ascent, Icon of the Seas) that anchored the yield recovery.
Q2 2025 by the numbers: 110% load factor, Adj EBITDA $1.9B (+19%), 40.8% margin, Adj EPS $4.38 (+36%); investment-grade ratings regained from all three agencies.
The stated long-term value engine: moderate capacity growth + moderate yield growth + disciplined cost control ('our proven formula').
Perfecta program (successor to Trifecta): 20% Adj EPS CAGR off 2024 and ROIC >=17% by 2027, while holding investment-grade metrics.
Newbuild pipeline 2025-2028 by brand/class: Star of the Seas, Celebrity Xcel, Icon 4, Oasis 7, plus Partner-brand Mein Schiff ships and the first Celebrity River vessel.
Owned/exclusive land-destination pipeline: Perfect Day CocoCay (open), Royal Beach Club Paradise Island/Cozumel/South Pacific, Perfect Day Mexico, Puerto Williams Antarctica hotel.
Q3 2025 by the numbers: record 112% load factor and 44.6% Adj EBITDA margin, the peak-season high point.
'Game-changing ships' lineup adds Celebrity Compass & Seeker river ships (2027) and Celebrity Xcite (2028) to the orderbook.
Private-destination strategy quantified: growing from 2 to 8 owned/exclusive destinations by 2028 (high-margin owned itinerary content).
FY2025 results: revenue $17.9B, Adj EBITDA $7.0B, Adj EPS $15.64 (+33%), 110% load factor, 39.2% margin.
2019->2025 trajectory: revenue $11.0B->$17.9B, Adj EBITDA $3.6B->$7.0B, Adj EPS $9.54->$15.64 (through the 2023 recovery trough of $6.77).
FY2026 guidance: +6.7% capacity, 1.5-3.5% net yield growth, Adj EPS $17.70-$18.10; double-digit revenue and earnings growth.
Reaffirmed on track to hit the Perfecta goals by 2027 while maintaining solid investment-grade metrics.
Extended orderbook: Icon 5 (2028) plus two Royal Caribbean 'Discovery Class' ships slated for 2029 & 2032.
Celebrity River Cruises scaling to 20 ships total by 2031 (10 more on order), a new growth vector beyond ocean cruising.
'Eight destinations by 2028': the full owned/exclusive land-experience portfolio shown together (CocoCay, Royal Beach Clubs, Perfect Day Mexico).
FY2025 ROIC of 18.0% (Adj operating income $5.24B / invested capital $29.2B) already exceeds the 17% Perfecta target two years early.
Q1 2026 by the numbers: Adj EBITDA $1.70B (+21%), Adj EPS $3.60 (+33%), 109% load factor, 38.2% margin.
FY2026 guidance refreshed to Adj EPS $17.10-$17.50 (capacity +6.7%, NCCx ~flat) after absorbing fuel and geopolitical headwinds.
FY2026 guidance walk: -$0.62 fuel and -$0.12 JV headwinds offset by +$0.14 of business outperformance, lower non-fuel costs and share repurchases.
New orders Icon 6 & 7 for 2029 & 2030 (the 2029 slot shifted from 'Discovery Class' to Icon-class vs the prior quarter); Icon 4 renamed Hero of the Seas.
Launch of 'Royal One' and 'Royal One Plus' co-branded Visa cards (issued by Bank of America) to deepen loyalty and onboard/pre-cruise monetization.
